data.title: Agios: FDA Approval of Aqvesme in Thalassemia Unlocks Transformational Market Opportunity, Supporting Buy Rating and Higher Target
data.description:
H.C. Wainwright analyst Emily Bodnar maintained a Buy rating on Agios Pharma today and set a price target of $62.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Emily Bodnar has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the recent FDA approval of mitapivat (Aqvesme) for thalassemia and its commercial potential. She views the broad label in both non‑transfusion‑dependent and transfusion‑dependent alpha- and beta-thalassemia as a transformative milestone, positioning Aqvesme as the only approved oral therapy for a wide spectrum of anemic patients who previously had few or no options. The strong Phase 3 data in ENERGIZE and ENERGIZE‑T, which met primary and key secondary endpoints in a sizable patient population, underpins confidence in both efficacy and market uptake. In her assessment, the required REMS program and black box warning for hepatocellular injury are manageable, given the clear monitoring protocols, the reversibility of liver events upon drug discontinuation, and the fact that many thalassemia patients already undergo routine liver testing due to other hepatotoxic treatments.

Emily also believes that the REMS and safety labeling are unlikely to materially slow adoption, as specialist prescribers are already accustomed to similar risk‑management frameworks and survey work indicates limited impact on prescribing behavior. The company’s existing commercial platform from Pyrukynd, combined with a tailored dual-brand strategy that separates the PKD and thalassemia indications, is expected to support an efficient and effective launch. With product availability anticipated following REMS implementation, and early prescription interest already possible, she sees Agios as well positioned to translate this regulatory win into growing revenues. These dynamics, together with the strengthened risk‑benefit profile and expanded addressable market, support her decision to maintain a Buy rating and increase the price target.

In another report released today, Bank of America Securities also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $34.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Edgewise Therapeutics: Differentiated EDG-7500 Profile, Late-Stage Cardiology Catalyst, and Expanding Pipeline Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Joseph Pantginis of H.C. Wainwright maintained a Buy rating on Edgewise Therapeutics, retaining the price target of $42.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Joseph Pantginis has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore both the clinical and strategic strength of Edgewise Therapeutics. He highlights that EDG-7500’s updated Phase 2 CIRRUS-HCM data show sustained clinical benefit across key hypertrophic cardiomyopathy measures, including biomarkers, functional status, symptoms, and quality of life, while maintaining stable left ventricular ejection fraction and avoiding clinically meaningful declines. The absence of treatment-related atrial fibrillation in the lower-dose cohorts, combined with preserved efficacy, supports a compelling risk-benefit profile and suggests dose flexibility as the program advances. In addition, the ongoing Part D of CIRRUS-HCM, which is testing a range of doses and biomarker-guided optimization, is expected to generate a robust exposure–response data set to fine-tune the upcoming pivotal trial.

Pantginis also emphasizes that the company is on track to initiate a Phase 3 trial for EDG-7500 by year-end 2026, which he views as an important value-creating catalyst. Beyond EDG-7500, he maintains a constructive stance on the GRAND CANYON program, citing prior sevasemten data that showed favorable separation on functional outcome curves versus Becker muscular dystrophy natural history, reinforcing confidence in forthcoming results by the end of 2026. He further interprets the advancement of additional pipeline assets, such as EDG-15400, as evidence of the company’s productive R&D engine and its ability to broaden its addressable market. Taken together, the differentiated safety/efficacy profile of EDG-7500, clear late-stage development path, and expanding pipeline lead him to support a Buy rating on EWTX shares.

In another report released today, Truist Financial also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $46.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Prolonged Safety Uncertainty and Eroding Differentiation Keep EDG-7500 at Hold
data.description:
Analyst James Condulis of Stifel Nicolaus maintained a Hold rating on Edgewise Therapeutics, boosting the price target to $16.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

James Condulis has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to both safety uncertainty and a more challenging competitive backdrop for EDG-7500. While recent data from Parts B and C at the 25 mg dose show encouraging signs of clinical activity without meaningful declines in ejection fraction or observed atrial fibrillation, the additional new-onset afib case in Part D reinforces lingering concerns stemming from the earlier afib signal. In Condulis’s view, the current afib incidence in the trial, even under cautious dosing and tight patient selection, appears elevated relative to published background rates in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and compares unfavorably to competitor data such as aficamten, which has not shown new-onset afib in a similar setting.

Condulis also emphasizes that the key questions around afib risk and overall safety profile are unlikely to be definitively resolved before longer-term, controlled phase 3 data, which are still several years away, leaving investors exposed to prolonged uncertainty. At the same time, EDG-7500 has moved to an individualized titration regimen, weakening one of its original points of differentiation as the cardiac myosin inhibitor class becomes more established and regulators grow increasingly comfortable with existing options. With the next major catalyst not until Becker muscular dystrophy data in late 2026, and with tempered expectations for that readout, Condulis believes the risk/reward is balanced rather than compelling at current levels. As a result, he sees sufficient potential to remain engaged with the story, but not enough clarity or differentiation to justify a more constructive rating at this time.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
